title: Define Project Scope and Deliverables
summary: Step‑by‑step guidance for documenting in‑scope and out‑of‑scope items, key requirements, assumptions, constraints, and the final deliverables within a Project Initiation Document (PID).
content: Identify what the project will produce and what it will not. List in‑scope items (e.g., UI redesign, user testing) and out‑of‑scope items (e.g., backend API development). Record any initial requirements, assumptions, constraints, and the permitted number of revision cycles. Clearly enumerate each deliverable with acceptance criteria and responsible owners.
can_answer: How do I document scope, assumptions, and deliverables in a PID?
examples: In‑Scope: UI redesign, user testing. Out‑of‑Scope: Backend API development.
warnings: Vague scope statements can lead to scope creep.
